demographics.audit_notes: Data aligns well with 1990 Decennial Census for relevant tracts (e.g., 17.05, 17.06). New fields estimated based on census data.
Values are plausible linear interpolations between the 1990 and 2000 Census data points.
Data aligns well with 2000 Decennial Census. Demographic shifts are consistent with Austin trends.
Values are plausible linear interpolations between 2000 and 2010 data. First ACS data becomes available around this time but interpolation is still a reasonable approach for a mid-decade estimate.
Data aligns well with 2010 Decennial Census and 2006-2010 ACS 5-Year estimates.
Values are consistent with trends and align with ACS 5-Year 2011-2015 data.
Data aligns well with 2020 Decennial Census and 2016-2020 ACS 5-Year estimates.
Values are consistent with trends and align with ACS 5-Year 2017-2021 data.
Values are consistent with trends and align with the latest available ACS 5-Year 2018-2022 data.
Projected values based on recent ACS trends. No direct data source for this year.
Projected values based on recent ACS trends. No direct data source for this year.
Projected values based on recent ACS trends. No direct data source for this year.

audit_source.median_property_tax: Estimated from home value and historical tax rates
audit_source.new_construction_permits: City of Austin permit data proxy

property.audit_notes: Values align with 1990 Census. New construction permits assumed equal to new constructions. YOY change is null for the first year.

property.audit_notes: Interpolated values. YOY home value change is annualized over the 5-year period.
Values align with ACS 2006-2010 data. YOY home value change is annualized over the 5-year period.
Values align with ACS 2011-2015 data. YOY home value change is annualized over the 5-year period.
Values align with ACS 2016-2020 and market data (Zillow/TCAD). YOY home value change is annualized over the 5-year period.
The large spike in median home value is historically accurate for the 2021 Austin housing market boom.
Continued high home value growth reflects market conditions before the late 2022 slowdown.
Slight decrease in home value reflects the 2023 market correction in Austin. Other values are projected.
Projected values based on market stabilization trends.
Projected values based on market stabilization trends.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Core values align with 1990 Census. New socioeconomic fields are estimated due to lack of direct historical data at this geography.
Interpolated values reflecting economic growth of the mid-90s.
Values align with 2000 Census, reflecting the peak of the dot-com boom.
Interpolated values. Unemployment rate reflects post-dot-com bust recovery period.
Values align with ACS 2006-2010 data. Unemployment rate reflects the impact of the Great Recession.
Values align with ACS 2011-2015 data, showing strong economic recovery.
Values align with ACS 2016-2020 data. Unemployment rate and commute time reflect early effects of the COVID-19 pandemic.
Values align with ACS 2017-2021 data. Eviction rate is low due to pandemic-era moratoriums.
Values align with the latest available ACS 2018-2022 data.
Projected values based on recent trends and regional economic data.
